int.description: Signed integer type, typically 32 bits on modern platforms

char.description: Character type, typically 8 bits, for ASCII and UTF-8 code units

bool.description: Boolean type with true and false values

void.description: Incomplete type for functions that return nothing and for generic pointers

auto.description: Placeholder type that is deduced by the compiler from the initializer expression (C++11)

std::string.description: Dynamic-length character string from the standard library with rich manipulation methods

std::vector.description: Dynamic array container with contiguous storage and amortized O(1) push_back

std::map.signature: template<class K, class V> class std::map
std::map.description: Ordered associative container mapping unique keys to values using a red-black tree

std::unordered_map.signature: template<class K, class V> class std::unordered_map
std::unordered_map.description: Hash-based associative container with average O(1) lookup

std::shared_ptr.description: Reference-counted smart pointer for shared ownership of dynamically allocated objects

std::unique_ptr.description: Owning smart pointer with exclusive ownership semantics and zero overhead

std::optional.description: Wrapper that may or may not contain a value, replacing nullable pointers

size_t.description: Unsigned integer type for sizes and array indexing

nullptr_t.description: Type of the nullptr literal, implicitly convertible to any pointer type
